name: Update by member ID
description: Documented success response (Sample JSON Response) for UpdateMemberDetails invoked with a MemberId. Loyalty Process Output: status=true, message=null, with outputParameters.outputParameters.results containing the updated member's MemberId and ContactId.
notes: Authoritative source: Loyalty Management Business APIs Developer Guide v67.0, "Update Member Details" process section (lines 23252-23325) plus the shared "Run a Loyalty Program Process" resource definition (lines 22588-22655). HTTP method POST and "Available version 56.0" come from the shared resource definition at lines 22598-22603 (the process value UpdateMemberDetails itself is noted available in v56.0 and later, line 8021). The documented Example URL uses v67.0 with program DemoProgram: /services/data/v67.0/connect/loyalty/programs/DemoProgram/program-processes/UpdateMemberDetails. IMPLEMENTER-DEFINED: the doc explicitly states "The request and response parameters vary depending on the input and output parameters configured for the UpdateMemberDetails process type in a Salesforce org" (line 23260-23261), so the inner fields of processParameters and the results objects are org-configured — only the outer envelope (processParameters wrapper on request; message/outputParameters/simulationDetails/status on response) is fixed. processParameters is the standard request wrapper (type: Loyalty Process Parameter Map). Response schema name: "Loyalty Process Output". Both fixtures use the documented field names/casing and the exact documented sample values. Some request field names are interchangeable (see "Loyalty Management Connect API Interchangeable Request Fields"). The doc shows NO error/empty-result example for this process, so no error fixture was created. No documented response-body property table beyond the JSON example for this specific process.
